meaning: n.震动,颤抖,动摇,不稳定,不坚定
examples: The shakiness in world stock markets did not help the dollar.
The quality of the cinematography is quite good, except for a little shakiness in the beginning.
Under certain velocities, locomotive transverse shakiness is so sever that it prevents locomotive raising speed further.
Still, no matter how charming the dialogue, the shakiness of the translation can break immersion from time to time.
Stocks largely reflected the shakiness of overleveraged industrials, and of course, financials.
